matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: GCK MODY2 loss-of-function model (GCK glucose-sensing set-point model)
GCK activating hyperinsulinism model (GCK activating enzyme model)
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a GCK glucose-sensing enzyme review thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to glucose phosphorylation, beta-cell glucose sensing, hepatic glycogen synthesis, MODY biology, and congenital hyperinsulinism context and GCK-MODY review context, Beta-cell glucose sensing. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed GCK MODY2 loss-of-function model dosage suggests this sample carries the GCK prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests a GCK glucose-sensing enzyme review thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to glucose phosphorylation, beta-cell glucose sensing, hepatic glycogen synthesis, MODY biology, and congenital hyperinsulinism context and GCK-MODY review context, Beta-cell glucose sensing. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support. This specific marker is curated as GCK glucose-sensing set-point model. GCK MODY2 loss-of-function model is curated as a GCK marker for GCK-MODY review context, Beta-cell glucose sensing. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the glucose phosphorylation, beta-cell glucose sensing, hepatic glycogen synthesis, MODY biology, and congenital hyperinsulinism context pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched GCK MODY2 loss-of-function model at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the GCK prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Can modify or tag glucose phosphorylation, beta-cell glucose sensing, hepatic glycogen synthesis, MODY biology, and congenital hyperinsulinism context.

methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a GCK glucose-sensing enzyme review thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to glucose phosphorylation, beta-cell glucose sensing, hepatic glycogen synthesis, MODY biology, and congenital hyperinsulinism context and GCK-MODY review context, Beta-cell glucose sensing. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support. An observed GCK variant places this sample in the curated GCK research context. The local GCK bundle is glucose-sensing enzyme review oriented. Interpret markers through exact allele class, zygosity, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context rather than as stand-alone deterministic calls. GCK variants can alter or tag glucose phosphorylation, beta-cell glucose sensing, hepatic glycogen synthesis, MODY biology, and congenital hyperinsulinism context; effect direction depends on allele class, tissue, dosage, and biological context.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a GCK variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus.
